when_to_use: Use the Present Continuous for actions happening right now, at this exact moment, or temporary ongoing situations happening around the present time (this week, these days).
when_to_use_ar: استخدم المضارع المستمر للأفعال اللي بتحصل دلوقتي في هذه اللحظة، أو المواقف المؤقتة اللي بتحصل حوالين الوقت الحالي (الأسبوع ده، هذه الأيام).
why_arabic_speakers_struggle: Learners often confuse this with the Present Simple (I work vs. I am working) because both can describe present-time actions in Arabic without a strict grammatical distinction. English requires choosing: habitual/general truth (Present Simple) vs. happening right now or temporarily (Present Continuous).

examples.en: They are working on a new project.
We are living with my parents these days.
examples.ar: احنا عايشين مع والدي هذه الأيام

examples.en: He is calling you right now — pick up!

common_errors.explanation: The main verb must have -ing added; 'am/is/are' alone is not enough.
'right now' signals an action happening at THIS moment, which requires Present Continuous, not Present Simple.
State/stative verbs (know, like, love, want, believe, need) almost never take -ing — they describe a permanent mental state, not an ongoing action.

practice_fill_blank.hint: 'need' is a state verb — use Present Simple, not -ing.

practice_fill_blank.sentence: They ___ (build) a new house this year.
practice_fill_blank.hint: Ongoing situation around the present time period.

quick_rule: Present Continuous = happening RIGHT NOW or a temporary situation around now (this week/year). NEVER use -ing with state verbs (know, like, want, need, love, believe, understand) — those stay in Present Simple even when talking about now.
quick_rule_ar: المضارع المستمر = بيحصل دلوقتي أو موقف مؤقت حوالين الوقت الحالي. أبدًا متستخدمش -ing مع أفعال الحالة (know, like, want, need).
mnemonic: -ING = 'It's happening, In progress Now, Going on.'
connect_to_speaking: Practice: look around you right now and describe 3 things happening — 'The fan is spinning', 'My neighbor is talking loudly', 'I am practicing English.' This forces real-time use of the tense, not memorized examples.
